steps: Find the growth rate in cm per month: the tree grows 69 cm every \(m\) months, so the rate is \(\dfrac{69}{m}\) cm per month.
Multiply rate by time to get total growth: \(\dfrac{69}{m} \times 12k = \dfrac{828k}{m}\) cm.

Evaluate every choice against \(\dfrac{828k}{m}\):
  • A: \(\dfrac{69m}{12k}\) — places \(m\) in the numerator and \(k\) in the denominator, inverting both conversions. Eliminated.
  • B: \(\dfrac{69k}{12m}\) — simplifies to \(\dfrac{23k}{4m}\), not \(\dfrac{828k}{m}\). Eliminated.
  • C: \(\dfrac{69km}{12}\) — multiplies by \(m\) instead of dividing by it. Eliminated.
  • D: \(\dfrac{828k}{m}\) — matches \(\dfrac{69}{m} \times 12k\) exactly. That matches choice D — D is correct.
